queue.title: Documentary: “The Courage to Be Open: MIT OpenCourseWare and the Democratization of Knowledge”
queue.description:
Produced by Chris Boebel and Brett Paci, and edited by David Ozug, this fifteen-minute documentary celebrates MIT OpenCourseWare’s origin, influence, and global reach.

This documentary is the second of MIT Open Learning's MIT Stories documentary series. View the first MIT Story documentary, "Full Circle: Paula Hammond at MIT" here: https://video.odl.mit.edu/videos/5444a783abb94d1fb773ff390d21a603

queue.title: Millions of WordPress sites just got hacked... again
queue.description:
Warp is the agentic development environment born out of the terminal. Download Warp for free today at → https://go.warp.dev/fireship

Someone spent $100k buying a massive collection of WordPress plugins and planted a backdoor in all of them. Naturally, CloudFlare stepped in with EmDash: a slop-forked WP alternative that promises to fix plugin security for good.

#coding #programming #wordpress

🔖

queue.title: Original Hello World in "B" Programming Language - Computerphile
queue.description:
B is the forerunner to C - but seemed lost - Angelo Papenhoff decided to change it and brought it back from the brink! Here he tries to recreate Brian Kernighan's original 'Hello World' with a few wrinkles!

Angelo's B compiler is here: https://github.com/aap/b
Angelo's emulators: https://github.com/aap/blincolnlights 

There's a type in the end of the video, it should of course read "Obsolescence
